8b83dbc98b247f17#0: Likely trigger: NVIDIA emits a standard otpauth URI with no `algorithm` parameter or with `algorithm=SHA1`; `parseOtpAuthUri()` maps both to SHA-1, and `handleCreateEntry()` rejects SHA-1.","Not likely incompatible: SHA-1, 6-digit, 30-second TOTP is supported by `generateTotp()` and by stored entry generation paths after insertion.","Could also trigger: any service emitting default-SHA-1 otpauth URIs, explicit SHA1/SHA-1, or raw Base32-only setup where the user leaves the UI algorithm dropdown at SHA-1. Custom formats such as Steam-style nonnumeric codes or unsupported digits/periods are separate incompatibilities."],"openQuestions":["What is the actual NVIDIA QR/otpauth payload? Capture it before making NVIDIA-specific claims.","Which policy does the product want: broad otpauth interoperability including SHA-1, or strong-only new-entry enforcement? The recognizer needs this decision.","Should HOTP URIs without `counter` be accepted as counter 0 or rejected/warned? Current code defaults to 0.","Should imports silently normalize invalid digits/period/counter, or report warnings/hard failures for malformed backups?"],"probableRootCause":["Code/protocol inference: the failure is repo policy mismatch in the direct add path, not NVIDIA cryptographic incompatibility.","`parseOtpAuthUri()` correctly treats omitted otpauth algorithm as SHA-1, but `handleCreateEntry()` then rejects SHA-1 even though the rest of the app can use it.","The embedded UI worsens the issue by defaulting to SHA-1 and posting it for every create request, including raw-secret entries."],"recommendedFix":["Recommended fix for interoperability: make SHA-1 a first-class supported algorithm everywhere, because otpauth compatibility depends on it. Update create/update policy, docs, and tests together.","Centralize parsing and normalization in one recognizer so create/import/UI cannot drift. Use it from `handleCreateEntry`, `handleImportOtpAuth`, `importPayload` if appropriate, QR scan handling, and paste handlers.","Canonicalize secrets once after validation: uppercase and strip whitespace, hyphens, and padding before encrypting/storing. Existing `base32Decode()` already tolerates those forms, but storage is not canonical today.","Return precise errors: distinguish `unsupported algorithm`, `algorithm disallowed by policy`, `invalid Base32 secret`, `missing secret`, `missing label`, and `conflicting URI/manual fields`.","Keep docs/tests as the policy source of truth. If SHA-1 is allowed, update `README.md`, `API.md`, and the SHA-1 rejection tests; if banned, update imports/schema/UI to enforce the ban consistently."],"risksAndRegressionPoints":["Allowing SHA-1 may conflict with the current documented/tested security policy. This is a product/security decision, not just a code cleanup.","Changing secret canonicalization should be tested against existing exports/imports; OTP semantics should remain the same, but exported text form may change.","Parser conflict behavior can surprise users if an entered secret and pasted URI disagree; surface the conflict instead of silently choosing one.","Import behavior currently silently defaults malformed digits/period/counter. Tightening it may break old backups; leaving it may hide wrong OTP metadata.","No actual NVIDIA payload was inspected, and no runtime tests were run in this read-only analysis environment."],"verificationChecklist":["Capture the raw NVIDIA QR text and check for omitted `algorithm`, `algorithm=SHA1`, or `algorithm=SHA-1`.","Submit the same text via `/api/import/otpauth`; it should work if the only issue is SHA-1 and the URI is otherwise valid.","Verify a generated code against NVIDIA after import to confirm digits/period/algorithm match.","Run `node --check src/worker.js` and the test suite after implementation.","Manually verify raw secret add, URI paste add, QR scan add, both secret+URI conflict handling, `/api/import/otpauth`, JSON import, encrypted import, TOTP display, TOTP verify, and HOTP generation."] }
